narrative: FY2023-24 is the first meaningful year for RPT benchmarking. SAIL is the governance concentration leader on investments, loans/advances, purchases, and sales, with 81.66% related-party investments, 1.15% loans/advances, 6.66% purchases, and just 0.06% sales. Tata is mixed: strong on sales at 12% but weak on purchases at 40% and high on related-party investments at 96%. Jindal sits between SAIL and Tata/JSW on purchases at 7% and investments at 89%, but is high on loans/advances at 100%. JSW shows high related-party concentration in investments, loans/advances, and sales. On sustainability capex, Jindal leads at 23.42%, ahead of Tata at 18%, while Tata and JSW tie at 100% R&D. The structural story is clear: SAIL’s standalone PSU operating structure reduces RPT intensity, while Tata/JSW/Jindal group ecosystems create more related-party flows that require stronger governance controls rather than simple ratio-based judgement.
